metadata.title: US Port Pilot Associations — Tariff Catalog
metadata.purpose: Foundation data for pilotage cost calculator — Port PDA sub-module

confidence_notes.high: Confirmed from published regulatory tariff or CFR — verify current year rate
confidence_notes.medium: Structure confirmed, rates approximated from historical filings or industry benchmark
confidence_notes.low: Association confirmed to exist, rate data not yet collected — needs web verification
confidence_notes.needs_verification: All rates should be verified against current published tariffs before use in production

metadata.verification_status: DRAFT — rate figures require validation against current published tariffs
metadata.next_action: Pull current tariff PDFs from each association website listed below

associations.name: New Orleans Bar Pilots (NOBRA)
associations.full_name: Associated Branch Pilots of New Orleans
associations.regulatory_authority: Louisiana Board of River Pilot Commissioners
associations.notes: Bills by river segment. Separate rates for Lower River (Head of Passes to New Orleans) and Upper River (New Orleans to Baton Rouge). Compulsory for all vessels over 1,600 GRT.

lower_river.billing_note: Included in base rate for New Orleans calls

upper_river.billing_note: Additional segment charge for Baton Rouge calls
upper_river.notes: Rate per 100 GRT for river miles above New Orleans — verify current rate

associations.todo: Download current tariff PDF from nobra.com and update GRT rates

associations.name: Houston Pilots
associations.full_name: Pilots for the Bay and Harbor of Galveston
associations.notes: Covers Houston Ship Channel (50-mile dredged channel). Pilots board at Galveston outer bar. Also covers Texas City turning basin and Galveston island wharves.
associations.todo: Verify current rates from houstonpilots.com

associations.name: Sabine Pilots
associations.notes: Sabine-Neches Waterway. Important for petroleum and chemical tankers. Beaumont is a major grain/agri port.
associations.todo: Find tariff schedule — check Texas Board of Pilot Commissioners website

associations.name: Corpus Christi Pilots
associations.full_name: Board of Pilot Commissioners for Corpus Christi
associations.notes: Major petroleum export port. Growing in grain/agri exports.
associations.todo: Find tariff — check Texas pilot commission or port authority website

associations.name: Lake Charles Pilots
associations.full_name: Board of Louisiana River Pilot Commissioners — Lake Charles
associations.regulatory_authority: Louisiana Board of River Pilot Commissioners
associations.notes: Calcasieu River shipping channel. Important for LNG and petrochemicals. Also grain.
associations.todo: Find Louisiana pilot tariff for Lake Charles — check LA Board of River Pilot Commissioners

associations.name: Mobile Bar Pilots
associations.notes: Mobile Bay. Important coal, grain, and forest products port.
associations.todo: Verify current rates from mobilebarpilots.com or Alabama Pilotage Commission

associations.name: Panama City Bar Pilots
associations.notes: Florida Panhandle port. Forest products, sand, phosphate.
associations.todo: Check Florida Board of Pilot Commissioners for tariff

associations.name: Tampa Bay Pilots
associations.notes: Major phosphate and bulk port. Tampa Bay is long pilot run (~30 miles from anchorage to berth). Pilot boards at Egmont Key sea buoy.
associations.todo: Verify current rates — Florida Board of Pilot Commissioners publishes tariffs

associations.name: Port Everglades Pilots
associations.notes: Major petroleum and container port. Less bulk commodity relevance but included for coverage.
associations.todo: Check Florida Board of Pilot Commissioners

associations.name: Jacksonville Pilots
associations.notes: St. Johns River port. Auto, bulk, and container. Important for forest products.
associations.todo: Find tariff from Florida Pilot Commissioners or JAXPORT

associations.name: Savannah Bar Pilots
associations.regulatory_authority: Georgia Ports Authority / Georgia Pilots Association
associations.notes: 3rd largest US port by TEU. 21-mile pilot run from sea buoy to berth on Savannah River. Important for bulk commodities — forest products, grain.
associations.todo: Verify current rates from savannahpilots.com tariff page

associations.name: Charleston Pilots
associations.full_name: Commissioners of Pilotage for the Port of Charleston
associations.notes: Major container and bulk port. Cooper River transit.
associations.todo: Verify rates from SC Pilotage Commission website

associations.name: North Carolina Pilots
associations.regulatory_authority: North Carolina Ports Authority / NC Pilotage Board
associations.notes: Cape Fear River port. Grain, fertilizers, coal. 26-mile river transit.
associations.todo: Find NC Pilotage Board website and tariff schedule

associations.name: Morehead City / Cape Fear Pilots
associations.notes: Phosphate and bulk port.
associations.todo: Confirm separate association or same as Wilmington NC pilots

associations.name: Virginia Pilots
associations.notes: Hampton Roads is one of the largest US bulk ports — coal, grain, and break-bulk. Pilots also serve James River terminals (Richmond, Hopewell) for additional segment fees.

surcharges.notes: Vessels transiting Chesapeake Bay outbound to ocean — additional fee
Terminal above Hampton Roads — per segment

associations.todo: Verify rates from Virginia Board of Pilots — they publish tariffs annually

associations.name: Maryland Pilots
associations.notes: Long Chesapeake Bay transit (190 miles from ocean). Pilots board at mouth of Chesapeake Bay (same as Virginia Pilots then handoff to Maryland at state line). Important coal, grain, and bulk port.

grt_tiers.notes: Higher due to long bay transit

associations.todo: Verify — Maryland has a long bay transit that significantly inflates pilotage vs. other ports

associations.name: Delaware Bay & River Pilots
associations.notes: Delaware Bay transit (~90 miles from sea buoy to Philadelphia). Also bills for river transit above Philadelphia to Trenton. Important petroleum, chemical, and grain port.
associations.todo: Find Delaware/Philadelphia pilotage commission tariff

associations.name: New York & New Jersey Pilots
associations.full_name: United New York/New Jersey Sandy Hook Pilots Association
associations.notes: Busiest US port by volume. Sandy Hook pilots board at Ambrose anchorage. Very high pilotage rates due to complex harbor and dense traffic.
associations.todo: Verify from NY/NJ Pilotage Commission — rates regulated jointly by both states

associations.name: Boston Pilots
associations.notes: Boston Harbor. Petroleum, bulk, and salt imports.
associations.todo: Find Massachusetts pilotage board tariff

associations.name: Portland ME Pilots
associations.regulatory_authority: Maine Board of Licensure for Pilots
associations.notes: Important petroleum import port. Forest products. Searsport is bulk/break-bulk.
associations.todo: Find Maine pilotage board tariff

associations.name: Puget Sound Pilots
associations.regulatory_authority: Washington State Board of Pilotage Commissioners
associations.notes: Covers all of Puget Sound. Long transit from Strait of Juan de Fuca to inner Sound ports. Rates vary by destination — Seattle rates differ from Tacoma which differ from Olympia.

surcharges.notes: Rates vary materially by destination — Olympia higher than Seattle; verify per destination

associations.todo: Verify rates by destination — WA Board of Pilotage publishes detailed tariff

associations.name: Columbia River Pilots
associations.notes: Columbia River Bar is one of the most hazardous bar crossings in the world. Specialized bar pilot service. River pilots take over for upriver transit to Portland/Longview. Major grain export corridor (PNW wheat/soy).

surcharges.notes: Portland vs Longview vs Kalama — different rates per segment; verify

associations.todo: Verify rates and segment structure from Oregon Board of Maritime Pilots

associations.name: San Francisco Bar Pilots
associations.full_name: San Francisco Bar Pilots / California Pilotage
associations.notes: Covers San Francisco Bay, San Pablo Bay, and inland delta routes to Stockton and Sacramento. Important for bulk grain and agricultural products going inland. Bar crossing surcharge applies.

surcharges.notes: Delta transit to inland ports — additional segment

associations.todo: Verify rates — California pilotage rates are regulated by CA Board of Pilot Commissioners

associations.name: Los Angeles Pilots
associations.notes: Largest US port complex. Container dominant but also handles bulk commodities.
associations.todo: Verify from California Board of Pilot Commissioners

associations.name: Great Lakes Pilotage
associations.full_name: Great Lakes Pilotage — USCG Federal Program
associations.notes: UNIQUE: Great Lakes pilotage is federally regulated under 46 CFR Part 401, NOT by state pilotage commissions. Rates set by USCG annually. Required for all foreign vessels transiting the St. Lawrence Seaway and Great Lakes districts. Rates published in the Code of Federal Regulations and updated annually in the Federal Register.

district_one.description: St. Lawrence Seaway to Lake Ontario boundary

district_two.description: Lake Ontario to Lake Erie (above Niagara)

district_three.description: Lake Erie, Lake Huron, Lake Michigan, Lake Superior

surcharges.notes: Pilot travel to vessel — varies by district and location
Minimum service fees apply — check 46 CFR Part 401

associations.todo: Pull current year rates from 46 CFR Part 401 — USCG updates annually in Federal Register

associations.name: Galveston-Texas City Pilots
associations.full_name: Pilots for the Bay and Harbor of Galveston (Galveston-Texas City)
associations.notes: Note: Houston Pilots and Galveston Pilots may be same association depending on port — Texas City has its own pilotage distinct from Houston Ship Channel.
associations.todo: Clarify relationship to Houston Pilots — same or different association? Check Texas Board of Pilot Commissioners

research_notes.summary: Catalog built from training knowledge through mid-2025. Rate figures for 'medium' confidence associations are approximations based on regulatory filing patterns and industry benchmarks — they represent plausible structures but MUST be validated against current published tariffs before use in any PDA calculator.
key_findings: US pilotage is compulsory for foreign-flagged vessels over ~1,600 GRT at most ports
Rates are regulated at state level (state pilotage commissions) except Great Lakes (federal/USCG)
GRT is the dominant rate basis; LOA used by some associations, DWT rarely
Rate structures: most use step tiers (GRT bands) with flat fee per tier, some use per-100-GRT formulas
Mississippi River (NOBRA) is uniquely complex — segment billing over 200+ river miles
Long bay/river transits significantly inflate pilotage: Maryland (Chesapeake), Columbia River, Delaware Bay all charge more per GRT than open-harbor ports
Great Lakes pilotage is hourly (not GRT-based) — federal 46 CFR Part 401 — unique in US system
Gulf Coast bulk ports (NOBRA, Houston, Mobile) are highest priority for PDA calculator accuracy
data_gaps: Low-confidence associations (marked 'low'): need web verification for actual rate figures
NOBRA: segment rates for Upper River (New Orleans-Baton Rouge) need current verification
Great Lakes: current year hourly rates need to be pulled from eCFR
Night/weekend surcharges: collected for some ports only — need comprehensive survey
Shifting fees: need systematic collection — important for busy ports where vessels move between berths

2. Pull 46 CFR Part 401 from eCFR for Great Lakes current year rates
3. Check Texas Board of Pilot Commissioners for Gulf Coast TX associations

5. Fill low-confidence associations with verified rates

priority_for_calculator: NOBRA (New Orleans/Baton Rouge) — #1 priority, most complex, highest tonnage
Maryland (Baltimore) — #5, long bay transit inflates cost, important to model correctly
Columbia River — #6, major PNW grain export
